## 1. Role Justification

The UX Writer / Content Designer owns all player-facing text across the game: button labels, tooltips, error states, tutorial copy, notification text, store descriptions, and in-game dialogue. For casual games, words on screen are a primary UX concern — poor microcopy increases confusion, reduces retention, and creates localization debt. This role ensures tone-of-voice consistency, reduces player friction, and structures content for efficient localization across all target languages.

**Gap Analysis:** The Creative Design division has 4 of 5 planned FTEs filled (Lead Game Designer, Senior Game Designer, Level Designer, Economy Designer). The UX Writer slot was inadvertently not assigned to a recruitment phase. This PSD corrects that omission.

**Span-of-Control Impact:** Lead Game Designer (Mei Watanabe) span-of-control becomes 5 direct reports (Sr. Game Designer + Level Designer + UX Writer + Economy Designer). This matches the recruitment plan's span-of-control table exactly and is within the ≤8 hard limit.

---

# Position Specification Document (PSD)

**Role:** UX Writer / Content Designer (G39)
**Role Family:** Creative / Design
**Seniority:** Senior (L3)
**Team:** Creative Design — Casual Games Studio
**Reports To:** Lead Game Designer (Mei Watanabe)
**Pipeline Stages:** 1, 2, 5 (participates in Stage 2 prototype reviews)
**Priority:** P2
**Compensation Band:** $110,000 – $145,000 base
**Document ID:** PSD-2026-G39-001
**Generated:** 2026-04-10T09:00:00Z
**Generated By:** CHRO Office (Dr. Evelyn Hartwell) — System Auto-Generation

---

## 1. Role Justification

The UX Writer / Content Designer owns all player-facing text across the game: button labels, tooltips, error states, tutorial copy, notification text, store descriptions, and in-game dialogue. For casual games, words on screen are a primary UX concern — poor microcopy increases confusion, reduces retention, and creates localization debt. This role ensures tone-of-voice consistency, reduces player friction, and structures content for efficient localization across all target languages.

**Gap Analysis:** The Creative Design division has 4 of 5 planned FTEs filled (Lead Game Designer, Senior Game Designer, Level Designer, Economy Designer). The UX Writer slot was inadvertently not assigned to a recruitment phase. This PSD corrects that omission.

**Span-of-Control Impact:** Lead Game Designer (Mei Watanabe) span-of-control becomes 5 direct reports (Sr. Game Designer + Level Designer + UX Writer + Economy Designer). This matches the recruitment plan's span-of-control table exactly and is within the ≤8 hard limit.

---

## 2. Required Competencies (Weighted)

| Competency                           | Weight | Minimum Threshold | Assessment Method               |
| ------------------------------------ | ------ | ----------------- | ------------------------------- |
| Game UX Writing                      | 25%    | ≥ 4/5             | Portfolio review + writing test |
| Player-Facing Copy Craft             | 20%    | ≥ 4/5             | Timed writing exercise          |
| Tone-of-Voice Consistency            | 15%    | ≥ 3.5/5           | Style guide audit exercise      |
| Localization-Ready Content Structure | 15%    | ≥ 3.5/5           | Structured content design test  |
| Mobile Game Experience               | 10%    | ≥ 3.5/5           | Interview + portfolio evidence  |
| Cross-Functional Collaboration       | 10%    | ≥ 3/5             | Behavioral interview            |
| Design Tool Proficiency              | 5%     | ≥ 3/5             | Practical exercise              |

---

## 3. Minimum Qualifications

- **Experience:** 5+ years game UX writing or content design in mobile gaming
- **Portfolio:** Shipped mobile game(s) with demonstrable player-facing copy ownership
- **Education:** BA/BS in English, Communications, Game Design, HCI, or equivalent practical experience
- **Tools:** Figma (component text workflows), content design systems, TMS familiarity (Smartling/Phrase)
- **Localization:** Experience structuring content for multi-language deployment (string key conventions, context annotations, text expansion awareness)

---

## 4. Compensation Band

| Component         | Range / Value                                 |
| ----------------- | --------------------------------------------- |
| Base Salary       | $110,000 – $145,000                           |
| Positioning       | 75th percentile of market for role            |
| Equity            | 0.05% – 0.12% (per seniority band)            |
| Performance Bonus | Up to 10% of base salary                      |
| Signing Bonus     | $5,000 – $15,000 (if leaving unvested equity) |
| Conference Budget | $3,000/year (GDC, UX Writing Conf)            |
| Tool License      | Full Figma, content design tool suite         |

**Recommended Offer Point:** $128,000 base (75th percentile midpoint) for a candidate meeting all must-haves with strong portfolio.

---

## 5. Sourcing Channels (Prioritized)

| Channel                          | Priority | Quality Weight | Rationale                                   |
| -------------------------------- | -------- | -------------- | ------------------------------------------- |
| GDC / UX Writing Conf speakers   | 1        | 1.5×           | Proven thought leaders in game UX writing   |
| LinkedIn (mobile game studios)   | 2        | 1.2×           | Direct sourcing from King, Supercell, Zynga |
| Portfolio sites (Content Design) | 3        | 1.1×           | Self-published work samples                 |
| Referrals (studio network)       | 4        | 1.5×           | High-signal, pre-vetted by trusted contacts |
| Dribbble / Behance (UX Writing)  | 5        | 1.0×           | Visual-text portfolios                      |

---

## 6. Assessment Battery

| Assessment                                                                    | Duration | Pass Threshold | Weight |
| ----------------------------------------------------------------------------- | -------- | -------------- | ------ |
| Portfolio Review (automated rubric)                                           | Async    | ≥ 3.5/5        | 25%    |
| Timed Writing Exercise (button labels, tooltips, error states, tutorial text) | 90 min   | ≥ 4/5          | 25%    |
| Tone-of-Voice Consistency Test                                                | 60 min   | ≥ 3.5/5        | 15%    |
| Localization-Ready Content Structuring                                        | 60 min   | ≥ 3.5/5        | 15%    |
| Simulated Panel Interview                                                     | 45 min   | ≥ 4/5          | 10%    |
| Behavioral / Culture Add                                                      | 30 min   | ≥ 3.5/5        | 10%    |

---

## 7. Success Criteria

### 90-Day Milestones

- Complete content audit of all player-facing text in current build
- Establish tone-of-voice guide aligned with Creative Director's visual pillars
- Rewrite all tutorial copy with measurably improved clarity (playtest score ≥ 4/5)
- Deliver localization-ready content structure for Phase 9 handoff

### 6-Month Milestones

- Reduce player support tickets related to UI confusion by ≥ 30%
- Complete content design system integrated with Figma component library
- All in-game copy passes localization readiness audit (zero hardcoded strings, full context annotations)
- Participate in Stage 2 prototype reviews with actionable copy recommendations

### 12-Month Milestones

- Tone-of-voice guide adopted as studio standard across all game text
- Localization efficiency improved by ≥ 20% (reduced translation queries, faster turnaround)
- Player-facing copy contributes to D1 retention improvement of ≥ 2pp (measured via A/B test)
- Mentored junior content designer or established content design best practices doc

---
